title: Creamy Uruguayan Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring fatty and pillowy empanadas filled with creamy beef, perfect for a post-workout meal, with a unique touch of rose water and a low-sodium friendly recipe.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and cold butter, using a pastry blender or fingers to work the butter into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the rose water, salt-free seasoning blend, and eggs to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and low-sodium beef broth to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Spoon about 1/4 cup of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
